result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would make empathy a universal and instinctive language—one that bridges divides of experience, ideology, and circumstance, allowing everyone to truly feel and understand one another’s joys, pains, hopes, and fears. 

Empathy, in this sense, wouldn’t erase difference or erase conflict, but it would remove the blindness that often fuels hatred, injustice, and indifference. It might lead to systems built on compassion, decisions made with collective wellbeing in mind, and a deeper recognition that our shared humanity is more fundamental than what sets us apart.

A world where empathy is innate wouldn’t be a perfect world—but it might be a much kinder, more just, and more connected one.
